reasoning: The Lean statement captures all components of the informal claim: q is a (positive) prime via `q.Prime` on ℕ — equivalent to positive prime in ℤ, which is the standard reading; `q ≡ 3 [ZMOD 4]` is Int.ModEq expressing q ≡ 3 (mod 4); `GaussianInt ⧸ span {↑q}` is exactly ℤ[i]/(q) since GaussianInt = ℤ[i] and span of a singleton is the principal ideal; `IsField` is the field predicate; and `∃ finR : Fintype _, Fintype.card _ finR = q^2` correctly expresses "has q² elements" (Fintype cardinalities being canonical when they exist). Using ℕ for q rather than ℤ is immaterial since the mod 4 condition forces positivity and (q) = (-q) as ideals. No hypotheses are added or dropped relative to the intended claim.
